description: Petition draft. From Manṣūr b. Salāma (Khan) or b. Ṣadaqa (Elbaum and Rustow independently of one another) to the Fatimid caliph al-Āmir bi-Aḥkām Allāh (1101–30 CE). In Arabic script. The petitioner lives in Sindiyūn (on the east bank of the Rashīd arm of the Nile, between Fuwa and Rashīd) in the province of al-Muzāḥamiyya. His father may have been a tax farmer (though Khan reads the word as ṣāmitan rather than ḍāminan) in an unidentified location called Maḥallat Salīm ibn Sahl. The petitioner's mother died, and he has been living in her house enclosure (dār). Manṣūr asks for a rescript (tawqīʿ) to be written on the back of his petition instructing the judge of al-Maḥalla to register the house enclosure as his property. He also asks for a copy for his own records. Information from Khan's edition. Pinholes in the margin.
description_he: טיוטת עצומה ממנצור בן סלאמה (או בן צדקה) לכליף הפאטמי אל-אמר בי-אחכאם אללה (1101-1130). בכתב ערבי. המעתיר מסינדיון. אמו נפטרה; הוא מתגורר בחצר ביתה. מבקש רשיון (תוקיע) לרשום את החצר על שמו אצל שופט אל-מחלה.
